era: 1990-1996 core. Alt Rock. E=6 -- melodic, hook-focused, alt rock. Seattle. Frontier Records.

theory_nodes.concept: Frontier Records enters at 231. Lisa Fancher's LA punk-to-alt-rock label connects the Seattle scene to West Coast independent tradition. Circle Jerks, T.S.O.L. to Flop: the label's trajectory.
theory_nodes.description: LA independent extends reach to PNW.

theory_nodes.concept: The commercial lottery: Flop and Candlebox occupied adjacent sonic territory. Candlebox got Atlantic and MTV. Flop got Frontier and critical respect. The gap is a signing-frenzy artifact, not a quality gap.
The best-kept secret: critical acknowledgment of quality combined with explanation of commercial failure. Flop is the engine's clearest example of craft that the commercial machine missed.

cross_references.relationship: E=6 continues. Sub Pop to Frontier. Melodic middle holds across two degrees.
E rises to 7. Roadrunner. Hard rock. The melodic middle gives way.

sps_pair.pairing_note: FIRST FALCONIDAE. E=6+E=7=E=13. Hooks meet the stoop. Melodic alt rock pairs with fastest animal on Earth.
